Typically this is an out-of-bounds error where the user has walked past the end of an array, but could also be an illegal instruction, illegal register, or other case. In rare cases, it’s possible for a hardware failure or system software bugs to materialize as XID 13. When this event is logged, NVIDIA recommends the following: 1. Run the application in cuda-gdb or the Compute Sanitizer memcheck tool , or 2. Run the application with CUDA_DEVICE_WAITS_ON_EXCEPTION=1 and then attach later with cuda-gdb, or 3. NOTE: The Compute Sanitizer memcheck tool instruments the running application and reports which line of code performed the illegal read.
